response:
Leapsome is generally **more expensive but broader/all‑in‑one**, while 15Five is **cheaper and more manager‑focused**; both use modular pricing, so your actual cost depends heavily on which modules you turn on and your user count.[1][3][4][5][6]

### 1. Headline pricing (public & 2026 comparison data)

**15Five**

- **Entry pricing (official docs)**  
  - **Engage** (surveys & basic engagement): **$4/user/month** (annual)[4]  
  - **Perform** (performance reviews, 1:1s, goals/OKRs): **$8/user/month** (annual)[4]  
  - **Total Platform** (engagement + performance + OKRs bundle): **$14/user/month** (annual)[4]  
- **Other market comparisons / reviews**  
  - Many 3rd‑party sites still quote **Engage from $4** and **Perform from $10–11/user/month**, and a full bundle at **$14–16/user/month**, all billed annually.[1][2][6][7]  
- **Free trial / contract**  
  - No free plan; typically **annual contracts**.[1][4][6]  

**Leapsome**

- **Entry pricing (multiple review sources)**  
  - Modular pricing starting around **$8/user/month** for core engagement & OKR features.[3][5]  
  - Typical full‑suite usage (surveys + performance + OKRs + learning) often sits **around $15–20+/user/month**, depending on modules and volume.[5][6][7]  
- **Modular & minimums**  
  - Modules (surveys, performance, OKRs, learning, etc.) often priced **$3–7/user/month each**; “all‑in” configurations are frequently estimated **~$24/user/month** with **~$6,000/year minimums** for dedicated support.[6][7]  
- **Free trial / contract**  
  - Usually **no free tier**, but they offer **demos** and sometimes trial access; pricing is typically **quote‑based with annual contracts**.[1][3][7][8]  

**Cost takeaway**

- For basic engagement only, **15Five is significantly cheaper** (from **$4** vs **Leapsome’s ~ $8**).[3][4]  
- For a complete suite (engagement + performance + OKRs), **15Five “all‑in” is usually ~ $14–16/user/month**, while **Leapsome all‑in is often closer to ~$20–24/user/month**, plus higher minimums.[6][7]  

---

### 2. Core feature comparison

#### Performance management

- **Both**  
  - Performance reviews / 360s  
  - Goals & **OKRs**  
  - Continuous feedback  
  - Review automation and templates[1][3][5][6]  

- **15Five** – performance with a manager‑rhythm focus  
  - Weekly **check‑ins** and status updates as a core workflow[1][5][6]  
  - Review cycles and self‑assessments integrated with OKRs[1][6]  
  - Strong **manager coaching** and enablement tooling (training, AI coaching on some plans).[1][4][6]  

- **Leapsome** – broader “people operating system”  
  - Robust **performance reviews** with integrated **competency frameworks** and promotion criteria.[1][5]  
  - Deep OKR alignment across teams, plus analytics tying goals to performance.[3][5]  

**Implication:**  
If you want **tight weekly discipline and manager habits**, 15Five is purpose‑built for that cadence.[1][5][6]  
If you want **structured, competency‑based reviews tied to broader HR processes**, Leapsome is stronger.[1][5]  

#### Employee engagement & surveys

- **15Five**  
  - **Engage** module offers pulse and engagement surveys with drivers and benchmarks.[4][6]  
  - Weekly check‑ins double as ongoing micro‑surveys on workload, morale, etc.[1][5][6]  
  - Prescribed **manager action plans** based on survey results.[6]  

- **Leapsome**  
  - Advanced **engagement surveys** with DEI analytics, lifecycle (onboarding/exit) surveys, and action planning.[3][5]  
  - More sophisticated analytics and segmentation are frequently cited strengths.[3][5][6]  

**Implication:**  
For **simple, manager‑linked surveys**, 15Five Engage is effective and cheaper.  
For **deeper analytics, DEI and lifecycle surveys**, Leapsome is more powerful but at a higher
